deep_dive.title: Predictive control of human pancreatic cell fate using a digital model of in vitro differentiation
deep_dive.abstract: The controlled generation of mature stem cell-derived islets (SC-islets) remains a barrier to scalable cell therapy for diabetes. Here, we develop a predictive digital model defining the cell-state-specific regulatory logic governing fate specification during human SC-islet differentiation. We integrate 400,603 cells from 9 original single-cell multiomic datasets and 52 public single-cell RNA-seq and ATAC-seq datasets across 4 cell lines and 7 differentiation protocols. This model resolves transcriptional and chromatin accessibility dynamics while enabling time-resolved inference and in silico perturbation of cell-state-specific gene regulatory networks. We identify regulators across trajectories from endoderm progenitors to pancreatic exocrine and endocrine lineages, nominating new candidate regulators. Among these candidates, we validate previously unreported roles for STAT1 as an exocrine driver and ZEB1 as a dynamic regulator of early endocrine specification and later off-target serotonergic islet cell fate. This work provides an experimentally supported predictive framework and an interactive resource comprising 1,116 simulations to prioritize transcription factors and intervention windows for refining SC-islet differentiation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: digital model of cell differentiation enables in silico perturbation and virtual cell simulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 数字分化模型支持原位扰动和虚拟细胞模拟
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 数字分化模型支持原位扰动和虚拟细胞模拟
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 数字分化模型支持原位扰动和虚拟细胞模拟
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops a predictive digital model of human pancreatic cell differentiation from 400K+ single-cell multiomic data, enabling time-resolved inference and in silico perturbation of gene regulatory networks to control cell fate specification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对干细胞分化难以控制的瓶颈，整合了超过40万个单细胞多组学数据，构建了一个预测性数字模型。该模型能够解析转录和染色质动态，支持时间分辨推断和细胞状态特定基因调控网络的原位扰动。通过在计算机中模拟扰动，研究者识别出沿内胚层祖细胞到成熟胰岛细胞轨迹的关键调控因子，为糖尿病细胞治疗的可扩展生产提供了理论指导。

deep_dive.title: Mapping Tumor-Microenvironment dependencies with TMEformer: A spatial foundation framework enabling in silico perturbation
deep_dive.abstract: Despite the fundamental role of spatial context in driving tumor progression, most current computational models for virtual perturbation have largely overlooked its importance. Here, we introduce TMEformer, a tumor microenvironment-aware deep learning framework that leverages high-resolution spatial transcriptomics to jointly model intrinsic tumor cell programs and local microenvironmental signals by explicitly incorporating spatial architecture. Validated across diverse tumor spatial transcriptomic cohorts, TMEformer enables virtual perturbations that capture functional dependencies within local cellular ecosystems. Despite being trained on cancer-specific spatial datasets, TMEformer outperforms baseline models pretrained on large-scale corpora in capturing key tumor transitions, including lineage plasticity and the emergence of therapy resistance. Systematic perturbation analyses prioritize tumor-intrinsic transcription factors and TME-derived ligands that drive disease progression, recovering established regulators and revealing novel candidates. Furthermore, TME-derived embeddings improve the spatial stratification of tumor cells and align more closely with pathological architecture. Together, TMEformer establishes a general framework for modeling tumors as spatially coupled, perturbable ecosystems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: enables in silico perturbation in tumor microenvironment with spatial transcriptomics
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用空间转录组实现肿瘤微环境中的计算机模拟扰动
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用空间转录组实现肿瘤微环境中的计算机模拟扰动

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TMEformer, a spatial foundation framework that models tumor cell programs and microenvironmental signals to enable virtual perturbations and capture functional dependencies.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有虚拟扰动模型忽略空间背景的问题，提出肿瘤微环境感知的深度学习框架TMEformer。通过整合高分辨率空间转录组，显式建模空间架构，联合建模肿瘤内在程序与局部微环境信号。在多种肿瘤空间转录组数据上验证，实现了虚拟扰动并捕获了细胞生态系统的功能依赖关系。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Virtual cell models for drug or genetic perturbation

deep_dive.title: CAR T cell foundation model predicts immunotherapy response
deep_dive.abstract: Single-cell transcriptomics resolves CAR T-cell states, yet translating heterogeneous cellular signals into patient-level therapeutic response remains challenging. Existing studies primarily identify response-associated genes or cell populations through experimental and statistical analyses, but few predictive frameworks integrate gene-level structure with clinical outcomes. Here, we present gANCHOR, a T-cell foundation model built on a hierarchical hypergraph attention framework combining biologically informed representation learning with patient-level response prediction. By encoding gene-pathway relationships, gANCHOR learns pathway-aware cell embeddings that improve biological conservation and batch robustness. A cell-to-patient attention module then aggregates cellular information to infer therapeutic response. Across benchmark datasets, gANCHOR achieved the strongest overall performance in biological conservation and batch-correction assessments. In response prediction across 161 patients from five CAR T-cell studies, gANCHOR achieved an F1 score of 0.87, outperforming benchmarked single-cell foundation models. gANCHOR also identified reproducible response- and non-response-associated gene programs, providing interpretable biological insights into CAR T-cell efficacy and resistance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: predicts CAR T immunotherapy response from single-cell transcriptomics using a T-cell foundation model, directly addressing perturbation response prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用T细胞基础模型从单细胞转录组预测CAR T免疫治疗响应，直接对应扰动响应预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用T细胞基础模型从单细胞转录组预测CAR T免疫治疗响应，直接对应扰动响应预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用T细胞基础模型从单细胞转录组预测CAR T免疫治疗响应，直接对应扰动响应预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: gANCHOR uses a T-cell foundation model with hierarchical hypergraph attention to learn pathway-aware cell embeddings and aggregate to patient-level CAR T immunotherapy response prediction, achieving accurate and interpretable predictions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 单细胞转录组虽能解析CAR T细胞状态，但将其转化为患者疗效预测仍具挑战。本文提出gANCHOR，一种基于分层超图注意力的T细胞基础模型，通过编码基因-通路关系学习通路感知的细胞嵌入，并利用细胞到患者注意力模块聚合预测治疗反应。在多个临床试验数据上，该模型在预测免疫治疗响应方面表现优异，且具有良好的生物学解释性。该方法为基于单细胞数据的扰动响应预测提供了有效范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 单细胞转录组虽能解析CAR T细胞状态，但将其转化为患者疗效预测仍具挑战。本文提出gANCHOR，一种基于分层超图注意力的T细胞基础模型，通过编码基因-通路关系学习通路感知的细胞嵌入，并利用细胞到患者注意力模块聚合预测治疗反应。在多个临床试验数据上，该模型在预测免疫治疗响应方面表现优异，且具有良好的生物学解释性。该方法为基于单细胞数据的扰动响应预测提供了有效范式。

deep_dive.title: Massively parallel characterization and predictive modelling of neuronal regulatory variation
deep_dive.abstract: Disease-associated variants reside frequently in noncoding cis-regulatory elements (CREs), yet their functional consequences remain poorly understood. We performed a large-scale lentiMPRA in human excitatory neurons, quantifying the impact of >46,000 naturally occurring variants across >27,000 candidate CREs near 524 disease-associated genes. These data improved regulatory variant effect predictions beyond state-of-the-art models. Significant allelic effects occurred at comparable rates across common, rare, and singleton variants, demonstrating that, within MPRA-measurable effects, population frequency carries limited information about per-variant regulatory impact. Variant effect detectability and magnitude were governed primarily by baseline activity of the enclosing regulatory element and local sequence context. Regulatory effects were distributed across numerous transcription factors rather than concentrated in master regulators, consistent with a combinatorial enhancer architecture. We establish a large-scale functional variant catalog and provide a complementary benchmark and resource for developing and evaluating models of noncoding regulatory variation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Massively parallel lentiMPRA to quantify variant effects and build predictive models for regulatory variants
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过大规模lentiMPRA实验量化遗传变异效应，构建调控变异预测模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过大规模lentiMPRA实验量化遗传变异效应，构建调控变异预测模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过大规模lentiMPRA实验量化遗传变异效应，构建调控变异预测模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Performed lentiMPRA on >46,000 variants, improved regulatory variant effect predictions, demonstrating that population frequency does not predict per-variant impact, directly addressing genetic perturbation response prediction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 非编码调控变异的功能注释是难题。该研究在人类兴奋性神经元中对超过46,000个变异进行了大规模lentiMPRA实验，量化了它们的调控效应。这些数据改进了调控变异效应预测模型。结果表明常见、稀有和新生变异的等位效应率相当，表明群体频率不能预测单个变异的调控影响。该工作直接贡献于遗传扰动下细胞响应的预测。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 非编码调控变异的功能注释是难题。该研究在人类兴奋性神经元中对超过46,000个变异进行了大规模lentiMPRA实验，量化了它们的调控效应。这些数据改进了调控变异效应预测模型。结果表明常见、稀有和新生变异的等位效应率相当，表明群体频率不能预测单个变异的调控影响。该工作直接贡献于遗传扰动下细胞响应的预测。

deep_dive.title: {Sigma}0-EvoCell: An AI-Native Ontology that Unifies Evolutionary and Cell Biology in Latent Space
deep_dive.abstract: Foundation models for biology achieve impressive pattern recognition on molecular sequences and single-cell transcriptomics, yet they fail to outperform simple linear baselines for predicting genetic perturbation effects, exposing a gap between statistical correlation and mechanistic understanding. This gap is compounded by an interface problem: biological knowledge lives in human-readable formats (FASTA, SBML, ontology triples) that must be lossily re-encoded before a neural network can reason about them. Here we introduce the Evolutionary Cell Ontology (ECO), an AI-native formal language built on the {Sigma}0 substrate that represents biological knowledge directly as vector-encoded relational graphs. ECO uses 16 structural operators that serve simultaneously as knowledge glyphs, tensor operations, and--critically--carry a dual semantics spanning both evolutionary and cellular timescales, so the same operator that denotes speciation at the phylogenetic scale denotes irreversible APC/C commitment at the cell-cycle scale. We define a Latent Space Communication Protocol (LSCP) that maps ECO graphs into the residual stream of large language models, enabling systematic auditing of the biological knowledge a model actually contains. We illustrate ECO across three domains using controlled simulations: (i) globin protein-family evolution across roughly 1.5 billion years, where ECO epistatic attention recovers long-range coevolutionary couplings and ancestral-state reconstruction reaches 82-91% accuracy graded by conservation class; (ii) mammalian cell-cycle dynamics, where a CDK-cyclin attention graph with GATE checkpoints and FUSE commitment nodes reproduces two full oscillatory cycles with a four-attractor phase portrait emerging without explicit programming; and (iii) latent-space alignment, where ECO embedding distance tracks divergence across 60 protein families (Pearson r = 0.50) and an illustrative LSCP audit projects that relational, multi-scale concepts are encoded far more weakly than sequence-level facts. ECO replaces the human-readability constraint with an AI-processing constraint and, in doing so, turns the opacity of foundation models into a measurable, navigable coverage map.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly addresses predicting genetic perturbation effects using an AI-native ontology
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接针对利用原生AI本体预测遗传扰动效应
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接针对利用原生AI本体预测遗传扰动效应
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接针对利用原生AI本体预测遗传扰动效应
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Evolutionary Cell Ontology (ECO) to represent biological knowledge as vector-encoded graphs, aiming to overcome the failure of foundation models in predicting genetic perturbation effects.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对基础模型在预测遗传扰动效应上不及简单线性基线的问题，提出了基于Σ0底层的原生AI本体ECO。该本体将生物学知识直接表示为向量编码的关系图，避免损失性重编码。通过统一进化与细胞生物学，有望弥合统计相关性与机制理解之间的差距。

quick_skim.title: AI-Augmented Adaptive Digital Twin Modeling for Brain Tumor Evolution Prediction and Treatment Scheduling
quick_skim.abstract: Brain tumor progression exhibits spatially heterogeneous growth, patient-specific treatment response, and complex interactions with surrounding anatomy, making accurate long-term prediction challenging. We propose an AI-augmented adaptive digital twin (DT) framework for brain tumor evolution prediction and treatment scheduling. The framework integrates an interpretable reaction--diffusion (RD) model, a 3D residual learning module for model-form correction, patient-specific DT updating during recursive rollout, and model predictive control (MPC) for constrained chemotherapy and radiotherapy scheduling. Experiments on 387 synthetic tumor trajectories with 120-step evolution show that the baseline RD model captures tumor location and overall temporal behavior but underestimates heterogeneous tumor burden during long-horizon prediction. Hybrid RD--residual modeling reduces masked voxel-wise mean squared error by 84.3% and increases Dice overlap by 43.5% relative to the RD baseline under dense simulated observations. Online DT updating further reduces mean squared error by 45.9% and improves Dice overlap by 9.6% compared with the non-updated hybrid model. In MPC-based scheduling simulations, the updated DT controller reduces final tumor burden by 22.4% relative to a fixed treatment schedule under the terminal-burden objective. Together, these results demonstrate a unified framework for patient-specific initialization, mechanistic modeling, adaptive learning, and constrained treatment optimization. Although validated using patient-data-informed synthetic trajectories rather than clinical longitudinal data, the proposed framework establishes a foundation for future translation to real-world adaptive treatment planning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: digital twin modeling of tumor evolution closely parallels virtual cell model concept
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 数字孪生建模肿瘤演化与虚拟细胞模型概念高度相似
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 数字孪生建模肿瘤演化与虚拟细胞模型概念高度相似
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 数字孪生建模肿瘤演化与虚拟细胞模型概念高度相似
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: An AI-augmented digital twin framework predicts brain tumor evolution and treatment responses, illustrating the virtual cell modeling approach.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 脑肿瘤演化预测需要考虑空间异质性和治疗响应。本文提出AI增强的自适应数字孪生框架，结合反应扩散模型、残差学习和模型预测控制，预测肿瘤演化并调度治疗。在387条合成轨迹上验证。该框架与虚拟细胞模型思想一致，可作为细胞水平数字孪生的先导。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 脑肿瘤演化预测需要考虑空间异质性和治疗响应。本文提出AI增强的自适应数字孪生框架，结合反应扩散模型、残差学习和模型预测控制，预测肿瘤演化并调度治疗。在387条合成轨迹上验证。该框架与虚拟细胞模型思想一致，可作为细胞水平数字孪生的先导。

quick_skim.title: CDS: Counterfactual Directionality Score for Structured Interventions in Spatial Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Quantifying directional influence between node populations is a fundamental problem in graph-based modeling, particularly in spatial biological systems where cell-cell interactions shape functional outcomes. Existing approaches based on attention, attribution, or correlation capture associations but do not provide a principled framework for evaluating directional effects under controlled perturbations. We introduce a framework for structured counterfactual interventions in graph-based models to estimate directional influence between node types. Our approach trains a Neighbor Influence Model (NIM) to predict node states from local neighborhoods and applies constrained interventions that modify neighborhood composition while preserving key spatial and structural properties. We define the Counterfactual Directionality Score (CDS), which measures the change in predicted node state induced by targeted perturbations, and provide a theoretical interpretation of CDS as a finite-difference measure of local intervention sensitivity. To obtain valid uncertainty estimates, we introduce a core-level bootstrap procedure that accounts for dependencies within spatial samples. Experiments on synthetic spatial graphs with known directional structure show that CDS recovers directional influence, remains well calibrated under null conditions, and is robust to confounding signals, while preliminary results on spatial transcriptomics data reveal biologically plausible and consistent interactions across tissue cores.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: counterfactual intervention framework for directional influence in spatial graphs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 空间图中方向性影响的反事实干预框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 空间图中方向性影响的反事实干预框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 空间图中方向性影响的反事实干预框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Counterfactual Directionality Score using structured interventions to quantify directional influence between node populations in spatial biological systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对空间生物系统中细胞间方向性影响量化问题，提出了基于反事实干预的框架CDS。通过训练邻居影响模型（NIM）预测节点状态，并施加约束干预修改邻域组成，从而估计节点类型间的方向性影响。该方法在空间转录组数据上验证，能够捕获功能依赖关系。为虚拟细胞模型中细胞间相互作用建模提供了可迁移的方法论。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对空间生物系统中细胞间方向性影响量化问题，提出了基于反事实干预的框架CDS。通过训练邻居影响模型（NIM）预测节点状态，并施加约束干预修改邻域组成，从而估计节点类型间的方向性影响。该方法在空间转录组数据上验证，能够捕获功能依赖关系。为虚拟细胞模型中细胞间相互作用建模提供了可迁移的方法论。

quick_skim.title: Medea: An AI agent for therapeutic reasoning across biological contexts
quick_skim.abstract: Therapeutic hypotheses can transfer across diseases but their relevance depends on biological context. The same target, perturbation, or treatment can produce different effects across cell types, disease states, genetic backgrounds, and patients. Therapeutic reasoning therefore requires methods that preserve context, test when evidence supports transfer, and identify where context-specific effects limit it. Although AI agents can perform therapeutic analyses, existing systems often fail to preserve biological context over long workflows, verify intermediate computational steps, or reconcile conflicting evidence across datasets and literature. Here, we present Medea, an AI agent for therapeutic reasoning across biological contexts. Medea executes multi-step analyses using biological tools, machine learning models, and literature retrieval while enforcing verification during planning, execution, and evidence synthesis. We evaluate Medea across 5,673 open-ended analyses in three domains: cell type specific therapeutic target nomination in five diseases and 29 cell types, synthetic lethality prediction in 7 cancer cell lines, and immunotherapy response prediction from multimodal patient profiles. Using a previously unpublished epistatic miniarray profiling screen performed under two DNA-damaging treatments, we evaluate Medea on predicting synthetic lethality among 238,046 gene-gene pairs in yeast. Medea accurately predicts these experimentally measured synthetic lethal interactions, indicating that its performance reflects biological relevance rather than information leakage from benchmark datasets. Across these evaluations, Medea improves performance over large language models, reasoning models, biomedical agents, and specialized machine learning models while maintaining low failure rates and calibrated abstention. These results show that verifiable AI agents can perform therapeutic analyses across biological contexts.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: therapeutic reasoning across biological contexts, handling perturbations and context-specific effects
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨生物情境的治疗推理，处理扰动和情境特异性效应
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨生物情境的治疗推理，处理扰动和情境特异性效应
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨生物情境的治疗推理，处理扰动和情境特异性效应
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Medea is an AI agent for therapeutic reasoning that preserves biological context, tests evidence transfer across cell types and conditions, and reconciles conflicting data, supporting virtual cell models for drug or genetic perturbation analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有AI代理在治疗推理中难以保持生物情境，且无法验证中间步骤和调和矛盾证据。本文提出Medea，一个跨生物情境的AI代理，能执行多步推理、保留上下文并验证计算结果。Medea在多个治疗推理任务上展示了有效的情境感知能力。这项工作为构建虚拟细胞模型中的扰动推理提供了可迁移的AI框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有AI代理在治疗推理中难以保持生物情境，且无法验证中间步骤和调和矛盾证据。本文提出Medea，一个跨生物情境的AI代理，能执行多步推理、保留上下文并验证计算结果。Medea在多个治疗推理任务上展示了有效的情境感知能力。这项工作为构建虚拟细胞模型中的扰动推理提供了可迁移的AI框架。

quick_skim.title: Generating whole-brain neural activity and behavior through unified latent dynamics
quick_skim.abstract: Understanding how high-dimensional neural activity and behavior emerge from shared underlying dynamics remains a fundamental challenge in neuroscience. Addressing this problem is key to enabling digital twins that can faithfully reproduce and predict the multiscale brain-behavior dynamics of living systems. Here we present NEBULA (NEural and Behavioral modeling through Unified LAtent dynamics), a generative framework that jointly models whole-brain neural activity and behavior. By applying NEBULA to brain-wide recordings from C. elegans, we identify a shared low-dimensional dynamical manifold that underlies both neural activity and behavior, enabling long-horizon generation and targeted in silico interventions. Perturbations of the learned dynamics reveal behaviorally relevant transition points, whereas steering interventions enable controlled manipulation of neural and behavioral states without retraining. These results establish a framework for linking brain dynamics to behavior in a living organism and provide a foundation for scalable virtual experimentation in neuroscience.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative model of whole-brain neural activity with perturbations in latent dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全脑神经活动的生成模型，包含潜在动力学扰动
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 全脑神经活动的生成模型，包含潜在动力学扰动
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 全脑神经活动的生成模型，包含潜在动力学扰动
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: NEBULA is a generative framework that models whole-brain neural activity and behavior through shared latent dynamics, enabling in silico perturbations for digital twin applications.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 理解高维神经活动与行为的共同动力学是构建数字孪生的关键。本文提出NEBULA，一个联合建模全脑神经活动和行为的生成框架，通过共享低维潜在流形实现长期生成和靶向扰动。在秀丽隐杆线虫全脑记录上验证了其保真度和可控性。该方法为扰动建模提供了可迁移的潜在动力学框架，可推广到细胞层面。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 理解高维神经活动与行为的共同动力学是构建数字孪生的关键。本文提出NEBULA，一个联合建模全脑神经活动和行为的生成框架，通过共享低维潜在流形实现长期生成和靶向扰动。在秀丽隐杆线虫全脑记录上验证了其保真度和可控性。该方法为扰动建模提供了可迁移的潜在动力学框架，可推广到细胞层面。

quick_skim.title: Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature
quick_skim.abstract: The single-cell literature catalogs cell states as validated marker-gene programs -- a sparse, compositional prior. Conventional embedding methods do not leverage this prior and learn cell-state structure de novo from the expression matrix, producing dense dimensions needing post-hoc interpretation and batch correction. Here we show coding agents can author single-cell embedding models directly from the literature. Given a scenario that focuses this literature lens on a chosen biological subdomain, the agent edits a structured Python template, curating named, literature-cited gene programs and composing them into axes, without a gene-set database, training, or sight of the data. Across mouse and human tissues these zero-shot embeddings are competitive in biological quality with conventional, foundation-model, and program-informed baselines, batch-robust by construction and reproducible across runs, complementing data-driven embeddings. Because each dimension is a named, cited gene program, the embedding is interpretable and auditable, and its composable axes can be steered into a developmental tree.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: single-cell embedding models from literature can serve as building blocks for virtual cell representations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用文献自动构建单细胞嵌入模型，可作为虚拟细胞表示的基础模块
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用文献自动构建单细胞嵌入模型，可作为虚拟细胞表示的基础模块
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用文献自动构建单细胞嵌入模型，可作为虚拟细胞表示的基础模块
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Coding agents automatically construct interpretable single-cell embedding models from literature, enabling zero-shot cell state representations that may support modeling cellular responses.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统的单细胞嵌入方法需要从数据从头学习，难以利用已有的文献知识。本文提出用编码智能体直接从文献中构建可解释的单细胞嵌入模型，无需训练或数据。该智能体根据用户指定的场景编辑Python模板，从文献中提取基因程序并组合成轴。在多个小鼠和人类组织上的零样本嵌入效果良好。这种方法可以为虚拟细胞模型提供先验知识，辅助扰动响应预测。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 传统的单细胞嵌入方法需要从数据从头学习，难以利用已有的文献知识。本文提出用编码智能体直接从文献中构建可解释的单细胞嵌入模型，无需训练或数据。该智能体根据用户指定的场景编辑Python模板，从文献中提取基因程序并组合成轴。在多个小鼠和人类组织上的零样本嵌入效果良好。这种方法可以为虚拟细胞模型提供先验知识，辅助扰动响应预测。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找与扰动响应预测/虚拟细胞相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: perturbation response prediction; virtual cell model; single cell perturbation; perturbation modeling; Predicting cellular response to genetic perturbations; Virtual cell models for drug or genetic perturbation; Deep learning methods for perturbation response prediction; Single cell perturbation response prediction.

quick_skim.title: CAR T cell foundation model predicts immunotherapy response
quick_skim.abstract: Single-cell transcriptomics resolves CAR T-cell states, yet translating heterogeneous cellular signals into patient-level therapeutic response remains challenging. Existing studies primarily identify response-associated genes or cell populations through experimental and statistical analyses, but few predictive frameworks integrate gene-level structure with clinical outcomes. Here, we present gANCHOR, a T-cell foundation model built on a hierarchical hypergraph attention framework combining biologically informed representation learning with patient-level response prediction. By encoding gene-pathway relationships, gANCHOR learns pathway-aware cell embeddings that improve biological conservation and batch robustness. A cell-to-patient attention module then aggregates cellular information to infer therapeutic response. Across benchmark datasets, gANCHOR achieved the strongest overall performance in biological conservation and batch-correction assessments. In response prediction across 161 patients from five CAR T-cell studies, gANCHOR achieved an F1 score of 0.87, outperforming benchmarked single-cell foundation models. gANCHOR also identified reproducible response- and non-response-associated gene programs, providing interpretable biological insights into CAR T-cell efficacy and resistance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: single-cell T-cell foundation model for immunotherapy response prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于免疫治疗反应预测的单细胞T细胞基础模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于免疫治疗反应预测的单细胞T细胞基础模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于免疫治疗反应预测的单细胞T细胞基础模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents gANCHOR, a T-cell foundation model using hierarchical hypergraph attention to integrate gene-pathway relationships and predict immunotherapy response from single-cell transcriptomics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对单细胞转录组预测免疫治疗反应问题，提出T细胞基础模型gANCHOR。利用层次超图注意力框架编码基因-通路关系，学习通路感知的细胞嵌入，并通过细胞到患者注意力模块聚合预测治疗反应。在多个数据集上验证了模型优于现有方法，弥合了单细胞信号与临床结果之间的差距。
